mission.title: gitea: Wildcard Protected Branch
mission.description: Wildcard Protected Branch. Requested by 43+ users.

steps.title: Check current gitea setup
steps.description:
Verify your gitea version and configuration:
```bash
gitea --version
```
This feature requires a working gitea installation.

steps.title: Review gitea configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant gitea configuration:
With the last version, you could only protect branches that exists. But I think it should also be possible to protect branches given as wildcard (similar to GitLab),
e.g.: `pu/*`, `release/*`, `feature/*`

steps.title: Apply the fix for Wildcard Protected Branch
steps.description:
Fix #2922.
Replaces #7093.

I have tried to make this work if we get wildcard protected branches as well.  maybe you want to have a look.

The new API endpoints:
```
GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/branch_protections   (Get list)
POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/branch_protections  (Create new)
GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/branch_protections/{branch} (Get a specific)
PATCH /repos/{owner}/{repo}/branch_protections/{branch} (Edit)
DELETE /repos/{owner}/{repo}/branch_protections/{branch}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Wildcard Protected Branch" is functioning correctly.
